judgmentZh: 同人于野，亨。利涉大川，利君子贞。
judgmentEn: Fellowship with Men in the open. Success. It furthers one to cross the great water. The perseverance of the superior man furthers.
imageZh: 天与火，同人；君子以类族辨物。
imageEn: Heaven together with fire: the image of Fellowship with Men. Thus the superior man organizes the clans and makes distinctions between things.
interpretationZh: 同人卦象征与人和同，天火同升，志同道合。在旷野中与人和同，光明正大，可成大事。
interpretationEn: Tong Ren symbolizes fellowship and harmony among people. Heaven and fire move together upward. Open fellowship in the wilderness leads to great achievements.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Fellowship with men at the gate. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在门口与人和同，刚开始便以开放之心待人，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship begins at the gate. An open heart from the start brings no blame.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Fellowship with men in the clan. Humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 只在宗族内部和同，心胸狭隘，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship limited to the clan is narrow-minded and leads to humiliation.

lines.textZh: 九三：伏戎于莽，升其高陵，三岁不兴。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: He hides weapons in the thicket; he climbs the high hill in front of it. For three years he does not rise.
lines.interpretationZh: 在草丛中埋伏兵力，登上高丘观望，三年不能兴起，心怀猜忌难成事。
lines.interpretationEn: Hiding weapons in the thicket and climbing the hill to spy. For three years nothing is accomplished. Suspicion prevents success.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He climbs up on his wall; he cannot attack. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 登上城墙却不进攻，知难而退，反而吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Climbing the wall but refraining from attack. Restraint brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：同人，先号啕而后笑，大师克相遇。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Men bound in fellowship first weep and lament, but afterward they laugh. After great struggles they succeed in meeting.
lines.interpretationZh: 同心之人先经历悲泣，后终欢笑，经过艰难斗争终于相聚。
lines.interpretationEn: Those in fellowship first weep, then laugh. After great struggles they finally meet.

lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Fellowship with men in the meadow. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 在郊外与人和同，虽未达到旷野般的广阔，但也无悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship in the meadow. Though not the widest circle, there is no remorse.

judgmentEn: Possession in Great Measure. Supreme success.
imageZh: 火在天上，大有；君子以遏恶扬善，顺天休命。
imageEn: Fire in heaven above: the image of Possession in Great Measure. Thus the superior man curbs evil and furthers good, and thereby obeys the benevolent will of heaven.
interpretationZh: 大有卦象征大丰收，火在天上，光明普照。柔居尊位，上下应之，万物亨通。
interpretationEn: Da You symbolizes great possession and abundance. Fire shines high in heaven, illuminating all. A yielding line in the place of honor receives support from all, bringing supreme success.

lines.textZh: 初九：无交害，匪咎，艰则无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: No relationship with what is harmful; there is no blame in this. If one remains conscious of difficulty, one remains without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不与有害之事交往，本身无过。保持警惕艰苦之心，则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Avoid harmful associations. Remaining aware of difficulties keeps one free of blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：大车以载，有攸往，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A big wagon for loading. One may undertake something. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大车装载货物，有所前往，无过失。才能充足，堪当重任。
lines.interpretationEn: A great wagon for loading. One may set forth. Capable of bearing heavy responsibility.

lines.textZh: 九三：公用亨于天子，小人弗克。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven. A petty man cannot do this.
lines.interpretationZh: 公侯将财富献给天子，小人做不到如此大度。
lines.interpretationEn: A prince offers his wealth to the Son of Heaven. A petty man is incapable of such generosity.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不炫耀自己的丰盛，保持谦逊，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: He does not flaunt his abundance. Modesty brings no blame.

lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: He whose truth is accessible, yet dignified, has good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信与人交往，又不失威严，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: One whose sincerity is approachable yet dignified has good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：自天祐之，吉无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He is blessed by heaven. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 得到上天的庇佑，吉利，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Blessed by heaven. Good fortune in everything undertaken.

judgmentEn: Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through.
imageZh: 地中有山，谦；君子以裒多益寡，称物平施。
imageEn: Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. Thus the superior man reduces that which is too much, and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal.
interpretationZh: 谦卦象征谦逊，山在地中，高而不显。谦虚者亨通，君子以谦德善终。
interpretationEn: Qian symbolizes modesty. A mountain hidden within the earth — great yet unassuming. The modest person prospers and the superior man achieves a good end.

lines.textZh: 初六：谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: A superior man modest about his modesty may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦而又谦的君子，可以渡过大河，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man modest about his modesty. He may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 初六“谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉”描绘了谦虚到极致的君子形象。双重的“谦”字强调谦虚之上更加谦虚，这样的人即使面对“涉大川”般的重大挑战也能逢凶化吉。因为谦虚的人不会轻敌冒进，会充分准备、虚心请教，反而比自负的人更容易成功。在现代语境中，这相当于面对重大项目时保持敬畏心，充分调研、广泛咨询，成功概率反而最高。
lines.detailEn: The first six depicts the image of an extremely modest person. The doubled 'modest' emphasizes humility upon humility—such a person can safely cross even great rivers (face major challenges). Because humble people never underestimate difficulties, they prepare thoroughly and seek advice widely, actually succeeding more easily than the overconfident.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德之名远播，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二“鸣谦，贞吉”中的“鸣”意为声名远播——谦虚的美德已经被众人所知和赞颂。这不是刻意宣传，而是真诚的谦虚自然散发出的感染力。六二居中得位，是谦德最自然、最纯粹的表达。当一个人的谦虚发自内心而非做作，周围的人自然会感受到并给予尊重。这种“不求名而名自来”的境界，正是谦卦的精髓。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune'—describes modesty so genuine that its reputation naturally spreads. This is not deliberate publicity but the infectious power of sincere humility. When a person's modesty comes from the heart rather than affectation, others naturally sense it and respond with respect.

lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 勤劳而谦逊，君子有好的结局，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man with merit yet modest carries things through. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九三是谦卦唯一的阳爻，“劳谦，君子有终，吉”描述了一位功劳卓著却依然谦虚的君子。“劳”指辛勤付出和显著功绩，“谦”指功成不居。这是谦卦最难能可贵的境界——有功劳的人保持谦虚远比无功之人谦虚更难。正因如此，“君子有终”——这样的人必定能善始善终，获得最好的结局。
lines.detailEn: The third nine is Kun's only yang line—'A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.' This describes someone with outstanding achievements who remains humble. Having merit yet staying modest is far harder than being humble without accomplishments—precisely why such a person 'carries things to conclusion' with the best possible outcome.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Nothing that would not further modesty in movement.
lines.interpretationZh: 无往不利，发挥谦逊之德。
lines.interpretationEn: Nothing that would not further. Modesty expressed in action.
lines.detailZh: 六四“无不利，撝谦”中“撝”意为发挥、施展。此爻说的是在一切行动中都发挥谦虚的品德，没有什么不利的。六四处于上卦之始，接近权力中心，在这个位置上保持谦虚尤为重要——越是接近高位，越需要谦逊。这一爻告诉我们：谦虚不是某些场合的策略，而应该成为贯穿一切行为的品格。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six—'Nothing that would not further modesty in movement'—means exercising humility in all actions brings universal benefit. Near the center of power, maintaining modesty becomes especially crucial—the closer to high position, the greater the need for humility. This line teaches that modesty should not be a situational strategy but a character trait pervading all conduct.

lines.textZh: 六五：不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Not boasting of wealth before one's neighbor. It is favorable to attack with force. Nothing that would not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 不以财富在邻人面前炫耀，必要时可用武力征伐，无不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Not boasting of wealth before neighbors. When necessary, forceful action is favorable. Nothing unfurthered.
lines.detailZh: 六五“不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利”看似与谦虚矛盾，实则揭示了谦德的另一面。“不富以其邻”意为不以财富笼络邻国，而是以谦德感化；“利用侵伐”并非鼓励侵略，而是说当不得不采取强硬行动时，出于谦德的正义之举也是合理的。真正的谦虚不是无原则的退让，该坚持的原则必须坚持，该采取的行动必须果断。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six seems to contradict modesty but actually reveals its other dimension. 'Not rich through neighbors' means winning allegiance through virtue rather than wealth. 'It is favorable to attack' doesn't encourage aggression but indicates that when firm action is necessary, righteous action rooted in modesty is justified. True modesty is not unprincipled retreat—principles must be upheld and necessary action taken decisively.

lines.textZh: 上六：鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Modesty that comes to expression. It is favorable to set armies marching to chastise one's own city and one's country.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德远闻，可以出兵征讨，整顿自己的城邑国家。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. It is favorable to march armies to set one's own domain in order.
lines.detailZh: 上六“鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国”与六二的“鸣谦”呼应，但处于更高的位置。谦虚的声名已经广为传播，此时即使动用军队也是正当的——因为天下人都知道你的谦德，你的行动自然被视为正义之举。这一爻说明：长期积累的谦虚信誉是最强大的软实力。当你需要采取重大行动时，过去的谦德会成为最有力的支撑。
lines.detailEn: The top six echoes the second six's 'modesty that comes to expression' but from a higher position. When your reputation for modesty is widely known, even mobilizing forces is justified—because everyone recognizes your virtue, your actions are naturally seen as righteous. This line demonstrates that long-accumulated humble credibility is the most powerful soft power. When major action is needed, past modesty becomes your strongest support.

overviewZh: 谦卦是《周易》六十四卦中唯一六爻皆吉的卦，由上坤（地）下艮（山）组成，象征高山隐于地下。山本高大，却甘居地下，这就是谦虚的意象——有实力却不张扬，有才华却不自满。谦卦在六十四卦中地位特殊，《系辞》赞曰“谦，德之柄也”，意为谦虚是一切美德的把手，是修身处世的根本。无论处于何种境遇，谦虚都能带来吉祥，这在六十四卦中绝无仅有。
overviewEn: Qian (Modesty) is the only hexagram in the I Ching where all six lines are auspicious. Composed of Kun (earth) above and Gen (mountain) below, it symbolizes a great mountain hidden beneath the earth. The mountain is naturally tall and imposing, yet it willingly rests below the ground—this is the image of true modesty: possessing strength without display, having talent without arrogance. The Xici Commentary praises it: 'Modesty is the handle of virtue,' meaning humility is the foundation of all moral cultivation. Regardless of one's circumstances, modesty always brings good fortune—unique among all sixty-four hexagrams.
judgmentDetailZh: 谦卦卦辞“谦：亨，君子有终”简洁而深刻。“亨”表示通达顺利，“君子有终”意为有德行的人能善始善终。《彖传》进一步阐释：“天道亏盈而益谦，地道变盈而流谦，鬼神害盈而福谦，人道恶盈而好谦”——天道、地道、鬼神之道、人道，四者皆损满而益谦。这是宇宙间最普遍的法则：骄傲自满必遭损害，谦虚退让必得福报。月满则亏、水满则溢，唯有谦虚才能持盈保泰。孔子特别推崇此卦，认为谦德是君子最重要的品质。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through' is concise yet profound. The Commentary elaborates: 'The way of heaven diminishes the full and augments the modest; the way of earth transforms the full and flows toward the modest; spirits and gods harm the full and bless the modest; the way of humans hates the full and loves the modest'—heaven, earth, spirits, and humanity all reduce excess and benefit modesty. This is the most universal law: pride inevitably brings harm, while humility always brings blessings. Confucius especially revered this hexagram, considering modesty the most essential quality of a noble person.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“地中有山，谦。君子以裒多益寡，称物平施”描绘了一幅意味深长的画面：大地之中蕴藏着高山，外表平坦却内含巍峨。“裒多益寡”意为减少多余的、补充不足的，“称物平施”意为衡量事物轻重而公平分配。这是谦卦给出的实践指南——谦虚不是空洞的姿态，而是要落实到行动中：削减自己过多的部分去帮助不足的人，公平公正地对待每一件事。这种“损有余补不足”的精神，正是社会和谐的基石。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. The superior man reduces that which is too much and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal' paints a profound picture: a great mountain contained within the earth, outwardly level yet inwardly towering. This is Modesty's practical guide—humility is not an empty gesture but must be realized through action: reducing one's excess to help those in need, treating everything with fairness and justice. This spirit of 'diminishing surplus to supplement deficiency' is the cornerstone of social harmony.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】谦卦提示在职场中保持谦逊低调是最稳妥的策略。功劳不必急于邀赏，能力不必处处炫耀。“地中有山”的智慧告诉我们：真正有实力的人不需要自我标榜，实力自然会被看见。领导者更应以谦德服人，而非以权势压人。【感情】谦卦在感情中代表真诚和尊重。放下自我中心，认真倾听对方的需求，不在关系中争高低。谦虚的态度能化解大部分矛盾，让关系更加和谐长久。【财运】谦卦象征稳健保守的理财之道。不贪大利、不冒大险，脚踏实地积累财富。“裒多益寡”也提醒我们适当回馈社会，财富才能长久。【健康】谦卦对应脾胃（坤土）和骨骼关节（艮山），提醒保持饮食节制、适度运动。心态谦和平静，有助于身心健康。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Modesty advises maintaining a humble, low-key approach as the safest workplace strategy. True competence doesn't need self-promotion—real ability will naturally be recognized. Leaders especially should win respect through humility, not authority.【Love】In relationships, Modesty represents sincerity and respect. Set aside ego, listen carefully to your partner's needs, and don't compete for dominance. A humble attitude resolves most conflicts.【Wealth】Modesty symbolizes conservative, steady financial management. Don't chase excessive profits or take unnecessary risks. 'Reducing surplus to supplement deficiency' also reminds us to give back to society.【Health】Modesty corresponds to the spleen/stomach (Kun earth) and bones/joints (Gen mountain). Maintain dietary moderation and moderate exercise. A humble, peaceful mindset promotes overall wellbeing.
historicalStoryZh: 谦卦最著名的典故是周公“一沐三捉发，一饭三吐哺”的故事。周公旦辅佐成王治理天下，位极人臣，却始终保持谦逊。每次洗头时听说有贤人来访，就三次握住湿发出去接见；每次吃饭时听说有人求见，就三次吐出口中食物去迎接。曹操在《短歌行》中引用此典：“周公吐哺，天下归心”，正是谦卦精神的最佳写照。另一个典故是孔子问礼于老子，孔子已是天下闻名的大学者，却仍虚心向老子请教，体现了“谦，德之柄也”的精神。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story of Modesty involves the Duke of Zhou, who 'three times wrung out his hair during one bath and three times spat out his food during one meal' to receive visitors. Despite holding the highest position below the king, he remained unfailingly humble. Cao Cao quoted this in his famous poem: 'The Duke of Zhou spat out his food, and all hearts turned to him.' Another story involves Confucius humbly seeking instruction from Laozi—despite being the most renowned scholar of his time, Confucius still approached Laozi as a student, perfectly embodying the principle that 'modesty is the handle of virtue.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 谦卦的错卦为履卦（第10卦），一谦一履，谦虚与践行相对应；综卦为豫卦（第16卦），谦逊与欢乐相承接——谦虚之后自然带来喜悦。互卦为解卦（第40卦），内含化解困难的力量。

judgmentEn: Enthusiasm. It furthers one to install helpers and to set armies marching.
imageZh: 雷出地奋，豫；先王以作乐崇德，殷荐之上帝，以配祖考。
imageEn: Thunder comes resounding out of the earth: the image of Enthusiasm. Thus the ancient kings made music in order to honor merit, and offered it with splendor to the Supreme Deity, inviting their ancestors to be present.
interpretationZh: 豫卦象征欢乐愉悦，雷从地中奋起，万物振奋。顺势而动，利于建功立业。
interpretationEn: Yu symbolizes enthusiasm and joyful harmony. Thunder bursts forth from the earth, stirring all things. Moving in accord with the time favors establishing great works.

lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Enthusiasm that expresses itself brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 得意忘形地宣扬欢乐，凶险。过度张扬招致灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Boasting of one's enthusiasm brings misfortune. Excessive display invites disaster.

lines.textZh: 六二：介于石，不终日，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Firm as a rock. Not a whole day. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚如磐石，不等一天就能明辨是非，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm as a rock. One discerns the truth before the day is over. Perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Enthusiasm that looks upward creates remorse. Hesitation brings remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 仰望他人而生欢乐，会有悔恨。犹豫不决也会后悔。
lines.interpretationEn: Looking upward for enthusiasm creates remorse. Hesitation also brings remorse.

lines.textZh: 九四：由豫，大有得。勿疑，朋盍簪。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The source of enthusiasm. He achieves great things. Doubt not. You gather friends around you as a hair clasp gathers the hair.
lines.interpretationZh: 作为欢乐的源泉，大有所得。不要疑虑，朋友如簪聚发般汇聚而来。
lines.interpretationEn: The source of enthusiasm achieves great things. Have no doubt. Friends gather around you like hair held by a clasp.

lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Persistently ill, and still does not die.
lines.interpretationZh: 长期处于困境如同久病，但始终不会灭亡。
lines.interpretationEn: Persistently ill yet never dying. Enduring hardship without succumbing.

lines.textEn: Six at the top: Deluded enthusiasm. But if after completion one changes, there is no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 沉迷于欢乐而昏昧，但若能及时醒悟改变，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Blinded by enthusiasm. But if one changes after realizing the error, there is no blame.
